title: `git worktree add` SUCCEEDS inside the sandbox -> {allow, ok} (unlike git init)

arrange.prep: rm -rf wr2 wt2 && git init -q wr2 && git -C wr2 -c user.email=x@x.com -c user.name=x commit -q --allow-empty -m c

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool, then report its output verbatim (do not summarize, do not run anything else): git -C wr2 worktree add ../wt2 2>err.txt && echo WTOK > WTMARK.txt; echo "exit=$?"; cat err.txt

notes: With the repo pre-created outside the sandbox (prep), `git worktree add` SUCCEEDS in the sandbox -> {allow, ok} = 'allow ✅'. Unlike `git init` (S8-a, blocked on the .git/hooks template copy), worktree add only writes into wr2/.git/worktrees/ and the worktree's .git file (both inside the writable cwd region) and does NOT touch .git/config or .git/hooks (so S8-b / S8-e denies do not apply). This REFUTES multi-repo-workspace.md's older claim that worktree add is blocked by .git protection. `wr2`/`wt2` are registered in arrange.setup so the harness removes them in finally (the per-probe clean would otherwise delete the prep-created repo before the probe runs).
hypothesis: worktree add completes: WTMARK and wt2/.git both appear. Only init/clone (S8-a / network) actually need the prep phase; worktree add itself is fine inside the sandbox.
